title: Chicken Curry with Coconut Milk
description: A fragrant and comforting Indian chicken curry featuring tender skinless thighs simmered in aromatic spices, light coconut milk, and fresh cilantro with potatoes. Great over basmati rice, with naan, or cauliflower rice to keep carbs low.
ingredients: 8 oz potato (1 large), peeled and diced small
instructions: Heat 1/2 tbsp olive oil in a large deep skillet over medium heat. Add 1/2 minced onion and 5 cloves minced garlic; sauté 1 minute until fragrant.
Add 1/2 tsp roasted cumin, 1 1/2 tsp garam masala, and 2 tsp curry powder. Mix well and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Place 6 skinless chicken thighs in the pan and season with 1 tsp kosher salt.
Add 1 chopped large tomato, 2 tbsp fresh cilantro, 1/2 cup light coconut milk, and 2/3 cup water. Stir to combine.
Cover and simmer on medium-low until chicken is cooked through, about 30 minutes.
Add 8 oz diced potato and 1/2 tsp kosher salt. Cover and simmer an additional 10 minutes, or until potatoes are tender.
Taste and adjust salt as needed. Serve over basmati rice, cauliflower rice, or with naan.
notes: Pressure Cooker Directions: Reduce water to 1/2 cup and cook the chicken after step 4 on high pressure for 15 minutes. Quick release, then add potatoes on high pressure for 5 minutes. Let it release naturally.
This recipe is naturally gluten-free. Ensure all spices are certified gluten-free if needed.
Light coconut milk works best; full-fat coconut milk can be used for a richer sauce.
If using boneless thighs, reduce stovetop simmer to about 20 minutes.
